segments.text: Then He spoke a parable to them, that men always ought to pray and not lose heart
saying:
There was in a certain city a judge who did not fear God nor regard man.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Now there was a widow in that city; and she came to him,
Get justice for me from my adversary.
segments.speaker: Widow

segments.text: And he would not for a while; but afterward he said within himself,
Though I do not fear God nor regard man,
segments.speaker: Judge

segments.text: yet because this widow troubles me I will avenge her, lest by her continual coming she weary me.
segments.speaker: Judge

segments.text: Then the Lord said,
Hear what the unjust judge said.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And shall God not avenge His own elect who cry out day and night to Him, though He bears long with them?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you that He will avenge them speedily. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will He really find faith on the earth?
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: The Parable of the Pharisee and the Tax Collector

segments.text: Also He spoke this parable to some who trusted in themselves that they were righteous, and despised others:
Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Pharisee stood and prayed thus with himself,
God, I thank You that I am not like other men—extortioners, unjust, adulterers, or even as this tax collector.
segments.speaker: Pharisee

segments.text: I fast twice a week; I give tithes of all that I possess.
segments.speaker: Pharisee

segments.text: And the tax collector, standing afar off, would not so much as raise his eyes to heaven, but beat his breast,
God, be merciful to me a sinner!
segments.speaker: Tax Collector

segments.text: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other; for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and he who humbles himself will be exalted.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then they also brought infants to Him that He might touch them; but when the disciples saw it, they rebuked them.
But Jesus called them to Him and said,
Let the little children come to Me, and do not forbid them; for of such is the kingdom of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Assuredly, I say to you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God as a little child will by no means enter it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: Jesus Counsels the Rich Young Ruler

segments.text: Now a certain ruler asked Him, saying,
Good Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?
segments.speaker: Ruler

segments.text: So Jesus said to him,
Why do you call Me good? No one is good but One, that is, God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You know the commandments: 'Do not commit adultery,' 'Do not murder,' 'Do not steal,' 'Do not bear false witness,' 'Honor your father and your mother.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And he said,
All these things I have kept from my youth.
segments.speaker: Ruler

segments.text: So when Jesus heard these things, He said to him,
You still lack one thing. Sell all that you have and distribute to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But when he heard this, he became very sorrowful, for he was very rich.

verses.title: With God All Things Are Possible

segments.text: And when Jesus saw that he became very sorrowful, He said,
How hard it is for those who have riches to enter the kingdom of God!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Who then can be saved?
segments.speaker: Those who heard

segments.text: The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Peter said,
See, we have left all and followed You.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: So He said to them,
Assuredly, I say to you, there is no one who has left house or parents or brothers or wife or children, for the sake of the kingdom of God,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: who shall not receive many times more in this present time, and in the age to come eternal life.
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: Jesus a Third Time Predicts His Death and Resurrection

segments.text: Then He took the twelve aside and said to them,
Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and all things that are written by the prophets concerning the Son of Man will be accomplished.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For He will be delivered to the Gentiles and will be mocked and insulted and spit upon.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They will scourge Him and kill Him. And the third day He will rise again.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But they understood none of these things; this saying was hidden from them, and they did not know the things which were spoken.

verses.title: A Blind Man Receives His Sight

segments.text: Then it happened, as He was coming near Jericho, that a certain blind man sat by the road begging.
And hearing a multitude passing by, he asked what it meant.
So they told him that Jesus of Nazareth was passing by.
Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: Then those who went before warned him that he should be quiet; but he cried out all the more,
Son of David, have mercy on me!
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: So Jesus stood still and commanded him to be brought to Him. And when he had come near, He asked him,
saying,
What do you want Me to do for you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Lord, that I may receive my sight.
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: Then Jesus said to him,
Receive your sight; your faith has made you well.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And immediately he received his sight, and followed Him, glorifying God. And all the people, when they saw it, gave praise to God.
